blocks.content:
Before you can use a variable, you need to create it.
This is called declaring a variable.
When you declare a variable, you tell the program two things:

• the name of the variable
• what kind of value will be stored inside it
Think of it like preparing a box and writing a label on it.
Here name is the name of the variable.
The word string tells the program that this variable will store text.
The text "Alex" is the value stored inside the variable.
Retype this code and check it.

Try to create a similar variable.
Name it `city` and store the text "Helsinki" inside it.
Text in C# must be written inside quotation marks. Without quotation marks the program will not understand that it is text.
